info.description:
This is a demo of the WID API that was provided by Oregon. 
The API was developed under WID 2.8 and is based on the OpenAPI 3.0 specification. 
The API provides access to a variety of workforce data, including Current Employment 
Statistics (CES), Quarterly Census of Employment and Wages (QCEW), and industry-occupation 
matrix data. The API is designed to be flexible and allows users to query data by various 
parameters such as area, period, industry code, etc.
info.title: Workforce Information Database 2.8 API

externalDocs.description: Find out more about the Workforce Information Database

pageSize.name: pageSize
pageSize.description: number of records returned in each page

stfips.name: stateFips
stfips.description:
2-digit state FIPS code
  * '00' - United States
  * '41' - Oregon

areatype.name: areaType
areatype.description:
2-digit code indicating type of area
  * '00' - National
  * '01' - State
  * '04' - County
  * '21' - MSA

periodtype.name: periodType
periodtype.description:
Code indicating the type of period (monthly, annual, etc)
   * '01' - Annual
   * '03' - Monthly

period.name: period
period.description:
2-digit code representing specific period.  
  Zero-padded number corresponding to time period (e.g. month, quarter).  Use 00 for annual data.

periodid.name: periodId
periodid.description: code used to look up the base and projection years

indcodty.name: industryCodeType
indcodty.description:
code describing the industry code type 
  * '05' - SIC
  * '10' - NAICS

occodetype.name: occupationCodeType
occodetype.description:
code describing the occupation code type 
  * '19' - SOC 2018
  * '45' - Wage Occupations

wagesource.name: wageSource
wagesource.description: code representing the source of the wage data

ratetype.name: rateType
ratetype.description: code identifying the type of wage rate

adjusted.name: seasonallyAdjusted
adjusted.description: flag indicating whether the data are seasonally adjusted or not

CesHoursAndEarnings.description: The CES object represents an employment estimate from the Current Employment Statistics program for a particular time period, industry, and area.

areaType.description: 2-digit code representing the type of area.  Common values include 00=US, 01=state, 04=county, 21=MSA

area.description: 6-digit FIPS code for the indiviual area

periodType.description: 2-digit code representing the type of period. 01=Annual, 03=monthly

industrySeriesCode.description: 8-digit code used by the Current Employment Statistics program to represent a specific industry.

adjusted.description: Indicates if record contains seasonally adjusted data. 1=adjusted, 0=not adjusted

benchmarkYear.description: Year that the data were last benchmarked

weeklyHours.description: Average hours worked per week for production workers

weeklyEarnings.description: Average weekly earnings for production workers

hourlyEarnings.description: Average hourly earnings for production workers

supppressHoursAndEarnings.description: Suppress hours and earnings data. 1=suppressed, 0=not suppressed

hoursAllWorkers.description: Average hours worked per week for all workers

CesEmployment.description: The CES object represents an estimate from the Current Employment Statistics program for a particular time period, industry, and area.  It contains an estimate of employment, and potentially other workforce indicators.

areaType.description: 2-digit code representing the type of area.  Common values include 00=US, 01=state, 04=county, 21=MSA

area.description: 6-digit FIPS code for the indiviual area

periodType.description: 2-digit code representing the type of period. 01=Annual, 03=monthly

industrySeries.description: 8-digit code used by the Current Employment Statistics program to represent a specific industry.

seasonallyAdjusted.description: Indicates if record contains seasonally adjusted data. 1=adjusted, 0=not adjusted

benchmarkYear.description: Year that the data were last benchmarked

employment.description: Number employed by place of work; actual number, not in thousands.
